purpose: COS Lending Selling WebApi is a .NET-based platform for managing loans in the marketplace lending ecosystem. It enables purchasing, changing, and managing loans, with features for calculating fees, handling transfers between accounts, and generating reports for various stakeholders.
businessFeatures: Fee calculation and collection (volume fees, Florida stamp tax, DMV fees, etc.)

apis.purpose: Retrieve loans with various filter options

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Core loan entity with all related details and status

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Actions performed on loans like purchases and fee collections

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Agreement between MPL and bank defining fee structures and terms

dataEntities.name: VolumeFee
dataEntities.description: Fees charged based on loan volume

messagingPatterns.name: TransferOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Ensures reliable processing of transfers between accounts

messagingPatterns.name: NotificationOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Manages external notifications about system events

messagingPatterns.name: BatchInitOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Triggers batch initialization for loan purchases

messagingPatterns.name: VolumeFeeOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Processes volume fee calculations and transfers

messagingPatterns.name: SQS Message Queue
messagingPatterns.description: Processes outbox messages for reliable delivery

findings.title: Sensitive information in repository
findings.description: Certificate files (CosLending.pfx) and license files (nsbLicense.xml) are committed in the repository. These should be stored securely in a vault and retrieved during deployment rather than being committed to source control.

findings.title: Hardcoded credentials in docker-compose file
findings.description: The docker-compose.yml file contains hardcoded database credentials. These should be externalized to environment variables or a secure configuration store to prevent credential leakage.

findings.title: Missing authorization checks
findings.description: PolicyTenantService sets tenants but doesn't validate if the user has the necessary permissions for specific operations. Consider implementing more granular permission checks based on operation types.
